_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local cron
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize LOCAL batch: Fort Worth (Crain, Sorrells) + Houston (Salinas, Peck, Evans-Shabazz) TX. Local tier; social-media/minutes/press protocol. Every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; null where no evidence; never inferred from party. Two factual corrections: Amy Peck is Jewish (not Christian); Carolyn Evans-Shabazz is a lay church member (not clergy).

profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored from FW council votes, candidate questionnaire, and local press (KERA, Fort Worth Report, Community Impact). Officially nonpartisan seat; self-identified Republican. Re-elected May 3, 2025 (71.6%).
profile.background: Real estate principal; former staff to FW Mayor Barr, Reps. Geren and Granger, and the G.W. Bush administration. Chairs FW Sister Cities. On the council Public Safety Committee.

michael-d-crain.notes_set: EVIDENCE-FINALIZED 2026-06-16 (local TX batch). Re-elected District 3 May 3, 2025 (71.6%); term to 2027. FISCAL (econ q2 TRUE): repeatedly voted against the adopted city tax rate seeking deeper cuts (2024, with Blaylock/Lauersdorf); campaigns on limited government / lower property taxes. POLICE (pub_justice q0 TRUE): 2025 questionnaire affirmatively pledged to support increased police/fire funding; sits on Public Safety Committee; helped select the new police chief (affirmative advocacy, not a routine budget vote). MANDATES (refuse_overreach q2 TRUE): Aug 17 2021 voted with the 5-4 majority to reject a mask mandate for city facilities. MARRIAGE (q4 TRUE): one of four members who declined to sign the June 2024 Pride Month proclamation, which (under FW's then-unanimous rule) killed it. NOTE the signal is mixed: in 2025 he was absent (Sister Cities trip) and reporting said he 'would have joined the photo' had he been present. No findable evidence on life, education, Christian-liberty advocacy, election integrity, immigration, or 2A (officially nonpartisan council seat; those issues seldom reach the dais) -> null. Caveat (not scored): arrested on a DWI charge Jan 16 2026.

4.note: One of four members who declined to sign the June 2024 Pride Month proclamation, killing it under FW's unanimous-sign-off rule. Mixed: 2025 reporting said he'd have joined the Pride photo if not absent.

2.note: Voted against the adopted 2024 tax rate seeking a steeper cut; 'not voted for the tax rates because I have not believed that city management has scrubbed the budget'; campaigns on lower property taxes.

0.note: 2025 questionnaire: pledged to support increased police and fire funding; serves on the Public Safety Committee and helped select the new police chief (affirmative advocacy).

2.note: Aug 17 2021 voted with the 5-4 majority to reject a mask mandate for city facilities (anti-mandate posture).

profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored from the Tarrant County DA office, campaign site, and press (KERA, Fort Worth Report, Bolts, NBCDFW). Republican; elected Nov 2022, term to Jan 2027; won the March 2026 GOP primary unopposed.
profile.background: Former Tarrant County Criminal Court No. 10 judge (25 years); prior felony prosecutor. Texas Tech undergrad and law.

phil-sorrells.notes_set: EVIDENCE-FINALIZED 2026-06-16 (local TX batch). Tarrant County DA since Jan 2023; current term to Jan 2027. PUBLIC JUSTICE (richest area): q0 TRUE - 'support the police... officers will know he has their backs,' endorsed by multiple police associations (affirmative advocacy). q1 TRUE - ran explicitly against Soros-style reform prosecutors; 'oppose all the low bails... keep those people in jail.' q2 TRUE - victim-advocacy statements + leads Texas in death-penalty trials (proportional punishment). q3/q4 null (no explicit statement on the 'public-health' framing or a Rom. 13:4 magistrate framing). BORDER: q1 TRUE - will 'aggressively prosecute criminal conduct by illegal aliens... detain them until they are deported'; q2 TRUE - same statement is anti-sanctuary (detain for removal). SELF-DEFENSE: q1 FALSE (DEVIATION FLAG) - his office filed a SCOTUS amicus in US v. Rahimi SUPPORTING the federal ban on gun possession by people under DV restraining orders (he sided with the firearm restriction, not the 2A challenger). ELECTION: created an Election Integrity Task Force and pursued the Crystal Mason illegal-voting re-conviction, but neither maps cleanly to the local cells (paper ballots / photo voter ID / Zuckerbucks) -> null. No findable evidence on life/abortion-prosecution stance, marriage/LGBTQ, family, or Christian-liberty advocacy (church membership only) -> null.

0.note: 'Phil Sorrells will support the police... He will fight to make officers a priority'; endorsed by FW Police Officers Assn, Dallas Police Assn, Arlington patrolmen, and others (affirmative advocacy).

1.note: Ran explicitly against 'progressive prosecutors backed by billionaire George Soros'; 'I would oppose all the low bails that are being issued right now, because we've got to keep those people in jail.'

2.note: Crime Victims' Rights Week: 'We stand with victims of crime... advocates for them'; office mission includes victim assistance; leads Texas in death-penalty trials (proportional punishment of capital crime).

1.note: Office will 'aggressively prosecute criminal conduct by illegal aliens and will make every effort to detain them until they are deported'; 'hold those people in state custody... until they are deported.'

2.note: Pledge to detain illegal aliens for removal is an anti-sanctuary enforcement posture (opposite of non-cooperation).

1.note: DEVIATION: his office filed a US Supreme Court amicus in US v. Rahimi SUPPORTING the federal ban (18 USC 922(g)(8)) on gun possession by persons under DV restraining orders - sided with the firearm restriction, not the 2A challenger. Was NOT endorsed by GOA (that went to his primary opponent).

profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored from the City of Houston site, campaign materials, recorded council votes, and press (Houston Public Media, The Texan, Style Magazine, Defender Network). Officially nonpartisan seat; consistently described as progressive/Democrat. Won the Dec 13 2025 runoff; sworn in Jan 6 2026.
profile.background: Litigation partner at Susman Godfrey; Boston College Law; first openly LGBTQ+ Latina on Houston Council and first Latina elected citywide in ~30 years.

alejandra-salinas.notes_set: EVIDENCE-FINALIZED 2026-06-16 (local TX batch). CORRECTION: won the Dec 13 2025 runoff (not 2023), sworn in Jan 6 2026; term to Jan 2030. MARRIAGE: q0 FALSE - openly lesbian, married, came out at the 2012 DNC 'to inspire LGBTQ youth,' board of the Greater Houston LGBTQ+ Chamber; q2 FALSE and q4 FALSE - LGBTQ-advocacy record + LGBTQ+ Victory Fund / LPAC / Houston LGBTQ+ Caucus endorsements (does not reject trans ideology; actively promotes LGBTQ identity). FAMILY: q0 FALSE - Houston Federation of Teachers endorsement + legal work against school policies (anti-school-choice alignment). POLICE (give credit where earned): pub_justice q0 TRUE - affirmatively backs HPD resourcing ('enough cars to patrol the city') + Boston hot-spot model; wants 'violent criminals off the streets' (not a defund stance). LOCAL CONTROL: refuse_overreach q0 TRUE - urged the city to seek a TRO against the State and 'vigorously defend the law we voted for' (asserting local control against state pressure). BORDER: q1 FALSE and q2 FALSE - voted AGAINST loosening Houston's immigration ordinance toward ICE; wanted STRONGER Fourth Amendment limits on ICE cooperation (pro-sanctuary-protections). No clean evidence on abortion (no PP/NARAL/EMILY's List endorsement found), Christian-liberty advocacy, election cells, fiscal deficit/balanced-budget, or guns -> null.

0.note: Openly lesbian, married to her wife; came out publicly at the 2012 DNC; board member of the Greater Houston LGBTQ+ Chamber. Does not affirm exclusive one-man/one-woman marriage.

2.note: LGBTQ+ Victory Fund / LPAC / Houston LGBTQ+ Caucus endorsements + LGBTQ Chamber board role document an LGBTQ-affirming (incl. trans) posture - does not reject transgender ideology.

4.note: Publicly promotes LGBTQ identity (came out 'to inspire LGBTQ youth'); LGBTQ-advocacy endorsements. Does not oppose LGBTQ promotion in public policy.

0.note: Houston Federation of Teachers endorsement (standard anti-voucher position) + legal work she frames as reversing 'discriminatory school policies.' Anti-school-choice alignment.
Affirmatively backs HPD resourcing - 'enough cars to patrol the city' + Boston hot-spot policing model; wants 'violent criminals off the streets.' Documented credit (not a defund stance).

Urged the mayor/city attorney to seek a TRO against the State (after Abbott threatened to pull $100M+): 'The mayor and city council must vigorously defend the law we voted for' - asserting local control against state pressure.

1.note: Voted against loosening Houston's immigration ordinance toward ICE; wanted stronger limits on ICE cooperation - opposite of mandatory-deportation enforcement.

2.note: Defended sanctuary-leaning Fourth Amendment protections for immigrants and vowed to strengthen them - supports, not opposes, sanctuary-style policy.

profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored from the City of Houston site, recorded council votes, and press (Houston Public Media, Click2Houston, The Texan). Officially nonpartisan seat; openly Republican-affiliated. In office since Jan 2020; current term to Jan 2028. NOTE: she is Jewish (her own statement), not Christian - Christian-liberty cells are null on that basis, not an oversight.
profile.background: Vice Mayor Pro Tem; Chair of the Public Safety Committee. Former Chief of Staff to CM Brenda Stardig. M.Ed. counseling psychology.

amy-peck.notes_set: EVIDENCE-FINALIZED 2026-06-16 (local TX batch). District A since Jan 2020; Vice Mayor Pro Tem and Public Safety Committee Chair; term to Jan 2028. FISCAL (econ q2 TRUE): fiscal conservative - on the 2024 flat tax rate, 'our tax base will leave if we make it more expensive'; FY2027 budget yes 'with reservations' with ~19 amendments and a threatened Prop A repeal if service doesn't improve. POLICE (pub_justice q0 TRUE): as Public Safety Chair championed the 2025 $832M HPD officer contract ('it's needed and well-deserved') and police pay/recruitment/technology (affirmative advocacy). BORDER (q2 TRUE): one of five NO votes against the April 8 2026 ordinance limiting HPD-ICE cooperation - voted to preserve police-ICE cooperation (anti-sanctuary); q1 null (no mandatory-deportation statement). CORRECTION: she is Jewish, not Christian - Christian-liberty cells null on that basis. No findable evidence on life, marriage/LGBTQ (HERO predates her tenure), family/education, election integrity, COVID/state overreach, or 2A -> null.

2.note: Fiscal conservative: 'our tax base will leave if we make it more expensive'; FY2027 budget yes 'with reservations' with ~19 amendments and a threatened Prop A fee repeal absent service improvement.

0.note: Public Safety Committee Chair; championed the 2025 $832M HPD officer contract ('needed and well-deserved') and police pay/recruitment/technology (affirmative advocacy).

2.note: One of five NO votes against the April 8 2026 ordinance limiting HPD-ICE cooperation - voted to preserve police-ICE cooperation (anti-sanctuary posture).

profile.religion: Baptist (Wheeler Avenue Baptist Church, lay member)
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored from the City of Houston site, recorded votes, endorsement records, and press (Houston Public Media, ABC13, Texas Tribune, Houston LGBT History). Officially nonpartisan seat; Democrat-aligned. In office since Jan 2020; term to Jan 2028. CORRECTION: she is a lay member of Wheeler Avenue Baptist Church, NOT a reverend/minister - do not score as clergy.
profile.background: PhD in Education (Texas Southern); retired Houston ISD teacher; first Black woman to chair the Houston Community College board; 2nd VP, NAACP-Houston.

carolyn-evans-shabazz.notes_set: EVIDENCE-FINALIZED 2026-06-16 (local TX batch). District D since Jan 2020; term to Jan 2028. CORRECTION: lay member of Wheeler Avenue Baptist Church, NOT clergy. MARRIAGE: q0 FALSE and q4 FALSE - endorsed by the Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus (2023), which endorses solely on LGBTQ-rights support (does not affirm one-man/one-woman marriage; does not oppose LGBTQ promotion). q2 (trans) left null - a single LGBTQ endorsement doesn't specifically document a transgender-ideology position. BORDER (q2 TRUE): voted FOR the April 22 2026 amended ordinance that removed Houston's ICE-limiting protections (to comply with the governor's $114M funding threat) - 'he should be happy with what was passed. I know that I am.' The recorded vote reduced sanctuary-leaning protections (note: compliance under fiscal duress, not stated anti-sanctuary conviction). PUBLIC JUSTICE: signed the Aug 2020 letter to Mayor Turner with ~25 police-reform/oversight demands (cite-and-release, oversight-board overhaul) - reform-side, does not meet the affirmative pro-police bar; no explicit defund statement either -> null. refuse_overreach left null (her immigration vote was compliance with the State, not asserting local control). No clean evidence on life, family/education specifics, Christian-liberty advocacy, fiscal votes, election cells, or guns -> null.

0.note: Endorsed by the Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus (2023), which endorses solely on LGBTQ-rights support - does not affirm exclusive one-man/one-woman marriage.

4.note: Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus endorsement documents alignment with LGBTQ advocacy - does not oppose LGBTQ promotion in public policy.

2.note: Voted FOR the April 22 2026 amended ordinance that removed Houston's ICE-limiting protections ('he should be happy with what was passed. I know that I am') - recorded vote reduced sanctuary-leaning protections. Note: compliance under the governor's $114M funding threat.
